effects.title: 3D Coverflow Carousel
effects.summary: Cards arranged on a rotating 3D cylinder, auto-spinning — the coverflow look.
effects.description: Panels are distributed around a Y axis with rotateY + translateZ inside a preserve-3d stage that rotates, placing items on a virtual cylinder you can spin.
effects.performance_notes: N panels each transformed once; rotating the stage is GPU-composited. Fine for ~6-12 panels.
effects.accessibility_notes: Decorative showcase. For a real content carousel, provide prev/next buttons, keyboard control, pause, and ensure off-screen panels are not focus-trapped (inert/aria-hidden). Stop auto-rotate under prefers-reduced-motion.

customization.name: panel count
customization.description: Number of panels (sets the angle step)

variations: Reflective coverflow with a CSS reflection
effects.ai_usage: Eye-catching media/product showcase. angleStep = 360/N; each panel i: rotateY(i*step) translateZ(radius). Wrap panels in a preserve-3d stage under a perspective container and rotate the stage (auto or via prev/next that change rotation by ±step). For production add controls, keyboard support, pause, and inert/aria-hidden on non-active panels. Disable auto-spin under reduced motion.

effects.title: 3D Flip Card
effects.summary: A card flips on hover/focus to reveal its back face in 3D.
effects.description: Uses perspective on the scene, transform-style: preserve-3d on the rotating inner element, and backface-visibility: hidden on two stacked faces to create the classic 3D card flip.
effects.performance_notes: Single rotateY transform; GPU-friendly.
effects.accessibility_notes: Make it flip on :focus-within too (not only hover) so keyboard users can reach the back. Back content stays in the DOM. Disable/instant under prefers-reduced-motion.
variations: Flip on click via a toggled class
effects.ai_usage: Use for flashcards, team bios, before/after, pricing toggles. Structure: .scene (perspective) > .card (preserve-3d, rotateY on hover) > .face.front + .face.back (both position:absolute, backface-visibility:hidden; back pre-rotated 180deg). Trigger with :hover AND :focus-within for keyboard access. For click-to-flip, toggle a class instead.

effects.title: Layered Depth Hover
effects.summary: Card layers separate along the Z-axis on hover for a pop-out parallax depth.
effects.description: Children are placed at different translateZ depths inside a preserve-3d card; tilting the card on hover makes the layers parallax apart, creating tangible depth.
effects.performance_notes: Transform updates on pointermove; throttle with rAF for grids.
effects.accessibility_notes: Enhancement only; flatten under prefers-reduced-motion.
variations: Add a parallax background image layer
effects.ai_usage: Premium product cards / posters with real depth. Card uses perspective + preserve-3d; each child sets translateZ via a data-depth attribute applied in CSS or JS. On pointermove, rotate the whole card (like tilt-card); the differing Z depths make layers shift relative to each other. Keep the closest layer (title/CTA) at the highest Z. Flatten when reduced-motion is set.
effects.source: DEPTH

effects.title: Rotating 3D Cube
effects.summary: A six-faced cube continuously rotates in 3D space, built from divs.
effects.description: Six faces are positioned with rotateX/rotateY + translateZ inside a preserve-3d container, which spins on a loop — a showcase of pure-CSS 3D.
effects.performance_notes: Six layers + continuous rotation; fine standalone. Avoid many cubes at once.
effects.accessibility_notes: Decorative; pause under prefers-reduced-motion.
effects.ai_usage: Eye-catching hero/showcase element. Each face is translated out by half the edge: e.g. front translateZ(s/2), back rotateY(180deg) translateZ(s/2), etc. The wrapper uses transform-style:preserve-3d and animates rotateX+rotateY. Half the edge length must equal translateZ. Wrap in a perspective scene. Pause under reduced motion.

effects.title: Animated Gradient Background
effects.summary: A smooth multi-color gradient that slowly drifts across the viewport.
effects.description: An oversized linear-gradient with background-size 400% and an animated background-position produces an endlessly shifting color wash — the ubiquitous SaaS hero background.
effects.performance_notes: Animating background-position is cheap; no JS. Long duration keeps repaints infrequent.
effects.accessibility_notes: Decorative. Keep foreground text contrast high across all gradient stops. Animation stops under prefers-reduced-motion.
variations: Pair with a noise overlay for texture
effects.ai_usage: Drop on a hero section or full page. Apply .animated-gradient to a full-size container; set background-size:400% 400% and animate background-position between corners. Use 3-4 harmonious colors. For readable text, overlay a semi-transparent dark layer or constrain text to a contrasted card. Keep duration long (12-20s) for an ambient feel.

effects.title: Animated Wave Divider
effects.summary: A gently rolling SVG wave that separates two sections.
effects.description: An inline SVG wave path sits at a section boundary; a duplicated path translates horizontally on a loop to make the crest roll — the popular section-divider flourish.
effects.performance_notes: Animating translateX on an SVG path is cheap. Use a wide path (≥200% width) and shift by one wavelength for a seamless loop.
effects.accessibility_notes: Decorative (SVG aria-hidden). Motion stops under prefers-reduced-motion (static wave).
variations: Layer two waves at different speeds/opacity for depth
Flip vertically for a top divider
effects.ai_usage: Use to transition between two colored sections. Place an SVG wave (path filled with --wave) at the boundary; make the path wider than the viewport and animate translateX by exactly one wavelength for a seamless roll. Layer 2-3 waves with different speeds/opacities for parallax depth. Mark the SVG aria-hidden and disable motion under reduced-motion. Match --wave to the next section's background.

effects.title: Aurora Background
effects.summary: Soft, blurred color blobs drift like northern lights behind your content.
effects.description: Several large radial-gradient blobs are blurred heavily and animated on independent paths over a dark base, creating a calm aurora/northern-lights ambience popular in modern landing pages.
effects.browser_support: All modern browsers (filter: blur widely supported).
effects.performance_notes: Large blur radii are GPU-intensive; limit to 2-3 blobs and avoid animating blur itself (animate transform instead).
effects.accessibility_notes: Decorative; keep content on a contrasted surface. Motion stops under prefers-reduced-motion.
variations: Tighter, brighter blobs for a nebula
Add grain overlay for film texture
effects.ai_usage: Use as a calm, premium hero/section background on dark UIs. Place the .aurora container behind content (position relative, content with higher z-index). Each blob is an absolutely-positioned blurred radial-gradient; animate their transform (translate) on long, offset loops. Keep blob count low for performance and pick 2-3 analogous-but-distinct colors.

effects.title: Dot Grid Background
effects.summary: An evenly spaced dotted grid drawn with a single repeating radial-gradient.
effects.description: One radial-gradient tile repeated via background-size produces a clean polka/dot grid — the understated backdrop seen in note apps and design canvases.
effects.performance_notes: Single tiled gradient — free.
effects.accessibility_notes: Decorative; keep dots subtle.
variations: Offset rows for a diamond layout
effects.ai_usage: Subtle backdrop for canvases, note apps, and minimal sites. Apply .dot-bg. The dot is radial-gradient(var(--dot) var(--r), transparent var(--r)); background-size:--size sets spacing. Adjust --r vs --size ratio for density. Works on light or dark by changing --dot.

effects.title: Floating Particles
effects.summary: Drifting glowing dots rise gently across the background, generated with a little JS.
effects.description: A small script spawns N absolutely-positioned dots with randomized size, position, and animation duration; CSS floats them upward in a loop for an ambient particle field.
effects.performance_notes: Keep particle count modest (≈30-60). Each animates transform/opacity only. For hundreds of particles, switch to canvas.
effects.accessibility_notes: Purely decorative; the script skips animation when prefers-reduced-motion is set (renders static dots).
variations: Connect nearby particles with lines via canvas
effects.ai_usage: Ambient atmosphere behind heroes. The JS loop creates COUNT dots with random left%, size, delay, and duration, appended to a relative container. CSS animates translateY upward + fade. Keep count low for DOM-based; escalate to canvas for dense fields. Always behind content (z-index) and behind the reduced-motion guard.

effects.title: Grid Lines Background
effects.summary: A crisp blueprint/graph-paper grid drawn entirely with a repeating gradient.
effects.description: Two linear-gradients (one per axis) with a fixed background-size create an infinite grid. A radial mask fades the edges for a focused, tech-y backdrop.
effects.browser_support: All modern browsers; the edge fade uses mask-image (well supported, prefix for older Safari).
effects.performance_notes: Pure CSS tiling, no animation — free.
effects.accessibility_notes: Decorative; keep grid faint so it doesn't reduce text contrast.
effects.ai_usage: Use behind dashboards, dev tools, and tech landing pages. Apply .grid-bg to a full container. Two repeating linear-gradients draw vertical+horizontal lines; --size controls cell pitch and --grid the line color/opacity. Add the radial mask to vignette the edges. Keep lines very low opacity over dark backgrounds.

effects.title: Mesh Gradient
effects.summary: Multiple overlapping radial gradients blend into a rich, painterly mesh.
effects.description: Layers several positioned radial-gradients in one background declaration to fake a mesh gradient (smooth multi-point color blends), the look popularized by Stripe and modern design tools.
effects.performance_notes: Static layered gradients are essentially free. No animation by default.
effects.accessibility_notes: Decorative; ensure overlaid content has its own readable surface.
variations: Animate background-position for a living mesh
effects.ai_usage: Use as a static decorative background for heroes, cards, or section dividers. Compose 3-6 radial-gradient(circle at X% Y%, COLOR, transparent N%) layers in a single background property; nearer color stops = tighter blobs. Pick a coherent palette. To animate, give it background-size>100% and animate background-position. Great fallback when true mesh-gradient CSS isn't available.

effects.title: Colored Glow Shadow
effects.summary: A soft colored halo under an element, echoing its own color for a premium glow.
effects.description: A large, blurred, low-opacity box-shadow tinted to match the element creates the 'colored shadow' look common on modern product cards and CTAs.
effects.performance_notes: Static shadow; trivial. If animated on hover, prefer transform/opacity changes.
effects.accessibility_notes: Purely decorative depth cue.
variations: Layer two shadows (tight + wide) for realism
effects.ai_usage: Adds a premium feel to cards, buttons, and product imagery. Use a colored shadow tinted like the element: box-shadow: 0 20px var(--spread) color-mix(in srgb, var(--c) 50%, transparent). Stack a smaller tighter shadow under a larger softer one for realism. Keep opacity low. Tint the shadow to match the element's dominant color.

effects.title: Flat Long Shadow
effects.summary: The flat-design 45° long shadow, built from many stacked text-shadows.
effects.description: Stacks dozens of 1px-incremented text-shadows in a darker shade to draw a solid diagonal long shadow — the iconic flat 2.0 / long-shadow style from the mid-2010s.
effects.performance_notes: Many shadow layers cost a bit of paint; fine for a heading/icon, not body text. Often generated by a preprocessor loop.
effects.accessibility_notes: Decorative; ensure the base text contrast holds on its own.
variations: Apply to box-shadow on a shape instead of text
Fade the shadow to transparent at the end
Use a Sass/PostCSS loop to generate the stack
effects.ai_usage: Use for retro flat-design headings, logos, and icons. The shadow is a comma-separated list of text-shadows at 1px,2px,3px… offsets in a darker hue. Generate the long list with a loop (Sass @for, PostCSS, or build-time JS) rather than hand-typing. Keep to large display text; avoid on paragraphs for performance.
effects.source: FLAT

effects.title: Gradient Border
effects.summary: A crisp gradient outline on a rounded element using the double-background trick.
effects.description: Layers a solid background (padding-box) over a gradient background (border-box) with a transparent border, so the gradient shows only in the border area — the reliable way to get gradient borders with border-radius.
effects.browser_support: All modern browsers. (border-image can't do rounded corners, hence this technique.)
effects.performance_notes: Static; free.
effects.accessibility_notes: Decorative border; keep inner content contrast independent of the border.
effects.ai_usage: The go-to for gradient borders WITH rounded corners. Set background: linear-gradient(var(--bg),var(--bg)) padding-box, var(--grad) border-box; with a transparent border of width --bw and your border-radius. Works on any element. For animation, register an @property angle and animate a conic-gradient. Don't use border-image if you need rounded corners.
effects.source:
Gradient Border

Rounded corners, crisp gradient edge.

effects.title: Marching Ants Border
effects.summary: An animated dashed outline that crawls around the element, like a selection marquee.
effects.description: Draws a dashed border using a repeating-linear-gradient background on each edge and animates background-position so the dashes appear to march — the classic selection/'cut here' marquee.
effects.performance_notes: Animates background-position on 4 gradients; cheap. (Animating border-style dashes is not smooth, hence the gradient approach.)
effects.accessibility_notes: Decorative selection cue; pause under prefers-reduced-motion. Don't rely on motion alone to indicate selection — pair with a label/state.
variations: Reverse direction on the opposite edges
Use as a focus indicator for drop zones
effects.ai_usage: Use for selected items, crop/cut UI, and active drop zones. Build four edge gradients (top/right/bottom/left) with repeating-linear-gradient of dash+gap, sized 1px thick on their axis, and animate background-position to march. Pause under reduced motion. Combine with a real state class so selection isn't conveyed by motion alone.
effects.source: Selected

effects.title: 3D Press Button
effects.summary: A chunky button with a solid bottom edge that depresses satisfyingly when clicked.
effects.description: A box-shadow acts as the button's 3D side; on :active the button translates down and the shadow shrinks, simulating a physical key press.
effects.performance_notes: Animates transform + box-shadow on :active; trivial cost.
effects.accessibility_notes: Real <button>; the press also triggers on keyboard activation focus state. Transition is short and not motion-sickness inducing, but is reduced under prefers-reduced-motion.
variations: Pill radius for a candy button
effects.ai_usage: Use for playful, game-like, or kid-friendly UIs. The 3D edge is a single box-shadow of height --depth in the --edge color. On :active, translateY(--depth) and remove the shadow so the button 'sinks'. Keep the transition fast (~60ms) for tactile feel. Pair --edge as a darker shade of --face.
effects.source:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
  <title>3D Press Button</title>
  <script type="application/json" id="effect-meta">
  {
    "id": "press-3d-button",
    "title": "3D Press Button",
    "summary": "A chunky button with a solid bottom edge that depresses satisfyingly when clicked.",
    "description": "A box-shadow acts as the button's 3D side; on :active the button translates down and the shadow shrinks, simulating a physical key press.",
    "theme": "buttons",
    "categories": ["button", "3d", "skeuomorphic"],
    "tags": ["3d", "press", "tactile", "box-shadow", "playful", "skeuomorphic"],
    "tech": ["css"],
    "era": "2020s",
    "difficulty": "beginner",
    "dependencies": [],
    "browser_support": "All modern browsers.",
    "performance_notes": "Animates transform + box-shadow on :active; trivial cost.",
    "accessibility_notes": "Real <button>; the press also triggers on keyboard activation focus state. Transition is short and not motion-sickness inducing, but is reduced under prefers-reduced-motion.",
    "customization": [
      { "name": "--face", "description": "Top face color", "default": "#22d3ee" },
      { "name": "--edge", "description": "Bottom edge color (darker)", "default": "#0e7490" },
      { "name": "--depth", "description": "How thick the 3D edge is", "default": "6px" }
    ],
    "variations": ["Pill radius for a candy button", "Larger --depth for arcade buttons", "Color-shift the face on hover"],
    "related": ["ripple-button"],
    "ai_usage": "Use for playful, game-like, or kid-friendly UIs. The 3D edge is a single box-shadow of height --depth in the --edge color. On :active, translateY(--depth) and remove the shadow so the button 'sinks'. Keep the transition fast (~60ms) for tactile feel. Pair --edge as a darker shade of --face."
  }
  </script>
  <style>
    :root { --face: #22d3ee; --edge: #0e7490; --depth: 6px; }
    html, body { height: 100%; margin: 0; }
    body { display: grid; place-items: center; background: #0b0d12; font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; }
    .press-btn {
      border: none; cursor: pointer; color: #042f3a; font-weight: 800; font-size: 1.05rem;
      padding: 0.85rem 2.2rem; border-radius: 12px; background: var(--face);
      box-shadow: 0 var(--depth) 0 var(--edge);
      transform: translateY(0); transition: transform .06s ease, box-shadow .06s ease;
    }
    .press-btn:hover { filter: brightness(1.05); }
    .press-btn:active { transform: translateY(var(--depth)); box-shadow: 0 0 0 var(--edge); }
    @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .press-btn { transition: none; } }
  </style>
</head>
<body>
  <button class="press-btn">Press</button>
</body>
</html>

effects.title: Animated Gradient Border Button
effects.summary: A conic gradient rotates around the button edge as a glowing animated border.
effects.description: Uses an animated @property angle driving a conic-gradient background, with a mask/inset technique so only the border shows. Falls back gracefully where @property is unsupported.
effects.browser_support: Best in Chromium/Safari/Firefox 2023+. @property animation of the angle degrades to a static gradient in older browsers.
effects.performance_notes: Animating a registered custom property (angle) is compositor-friendly. One element + one pseudo.
effects.accessibility_notes: Real <button>; focus-visible ring retained. Border rotation stops under prefers-reduced-motion.
variations: Pause rotation, spin on hover only
effects.ai_usage: Use for premium CTAs. Technique: the button background is a conic-gradient(from var(--angle), ...); a ::before with the solid --bg color is inset by the border width to reveal only the ring. Register @property --angle as <angle> and animate it 0→360deg. If you can't use @property, replace with a static linear-gradient border via the border-image or padding-box/border-box double-background trick.
effects.source:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
  <title>Animated Gradient Border Button</title>
  <script type="application/json" id="effect-meta">
  {
    "id": "gradient-border-button",
    "title": "Animated Gradient Border Button",
    "summary": "A conic gradient rotates around the button edge as a glowing animated border.",
    "description": "Uses an animated @property angle driving a conic-gradient background, with a mask/inset technique so only the border shows. Falls back gracefully where @property is unsupported.",
    "theme": "buttons",
    "categories": ["button", "border", "gradient"],
    "tags": ["gradient", "border", "conic", "glow", "animated-border", "outline"],
    "tech": ["css"],
    "era": "2020s",
    "difficulty": "advanced",
    "dependencies": [],
    "browser_support": "Best in Chromium/Safari/Firefox 2023+. @property animation of the angle degrades to a static gradient in older browsers.",
    "performance_notes": "Animating a registered custom property (angle) is compositor-friendly. One element + one pseudo.",
    "accessibility_notes": "Real <button>; focus-visible ring retained. Border rotation stops under prefers-reduced-motion.",
    "customization": [
      { "name": "--bg", "description": "Inner fill color", "default": "#11141c" },
      { "name": "--g1 / --g2", "description": "Gradient colors", "default": "#7c5cff / #2dd4bf" }
    ],
    "variations": ["Static gradient border (drop the animation)", "Thicker border for a frame", "Pause rotation, spin on hover only"],
    "related": ["ripple-button", "glow-pulse-button"],
    "ai_usage": "Use for premium CTAs. Technique: the button background is a conic-gradient(from var(--angle), ...); a ::before with the solid --bg color is inset by the border width to reveal only the ring. Register @property --angle as <angle> and animate it 0→360deg. If you can't use @property, replace with a static linear-gradient border via the border-image or padding-box/border-box double-background trick."
  }
  </script>
  <style>
    @property --angle { syntax: "<angle>"; initial-value: 0deg; inherits: false; }
    :root { --bg: #11141c; --g1: #7c5cff; --g2: #2dd4bf; }
    html, body { height: 100%; margin: 0; }
    body { display: grid; place-items: center; background: #0b0d12; font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; }
    .grad-border-btn {
      position: relative; border: none; cursor: pointer; isolation: isolate;
      padding: 0.95rem 2.4rem; border-radius: 14px; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 600; color: #fff;
      background: conic-gradient(from var(--angle), var(--g1), var(--g2), var(--g1));
      animation: spin 4s linear infinite;
    }
    .grad-border-btn::before {
      content: ""; position: absolute; inset: 3px; border-radius: 11px; background: var(--bg); z-index: -1;
    }
    .grad-border-btn:hover { filter: brightness(1.15); }
    @keyframes spin { to { --angle: 360deg; } }
    @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .grad-border-btn { animation: none; } }
  </style>
</head>
<body>
  <button class="grad-border-btn">Get started</button>
</body>
</html>

effects.title: Arrow Slide Button
effects.summary: A button whose arrow glides forward (and label nudges) on hover, signaling 'go'.
effects.description: On hover, a trailing arrow translates to the right while the label shifts slightly, a small directional micro-interaction that reads as forward motion.
effects.performance_notes: Transform on two inline elements; trivial.
effects.accessibility_notes: Real button; the arrow is decorative (aria-hidden). Motion removed under prefers-reduced-motion. Keep a focus ring.
variations: Arrow appears from 0 width on hover
effects.ai_usage: Use for 'Continue', 'Get started', 'Read more' CTAs where direction matters. Put the label and an aria-hidden arrow (→) inline; on hover translateX the arrow by --shift (and optionally nudge the label). Mark the arrow aria-hidden so screen readers don't read it. Keep the focus-visible ring and a reduced-motion guard.

effects.title: Fill Sweep Button
effects.summary: An outline button whose color sweeps in from one side on hover.
effects.description: A ::before pseudo-element scales/slides in from the left on hover, filling the ghost button with color while the label flips to a contrasting shade.
effects.performance_notes: Animates transform on a pseudo-element — GPU-friendly.
effects.accessibility_notes: Real <button>; color contrast valid in both states. Transition removed under prefers-reduced-motion (instant fill).
effects.ai_usage: Classic ghost-to-solid CTA. Button has transparent bg + colored border + label. A ::before is absolutely positioned, scaleX(0) with transform-origin:left, and animates to scaleX(1) on hover; put label text above it with position:relative;z-index:1 and switch its color. Change transform-origin and the scale axis to control sweep direction.
effects.source:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
  <title>Fill Sweep Button</title>
  <script type="application/json" id="effect-meta">
  {
    "id": "fill-sweep-button",
    "title": "Fill Sweep Button",
    "summary": "An outline button whose color sweeps in from one side on hover.",
    "description": "A ::before pseudo-element scales/slides in from the left on hover, filling the ghost button with color while the label flips to a contrasting shade.",
    "theme": "buttons",
    "categories": ["button", "hover", "outline"],
    "tags": ["hover", "fill", "sweep", "ghost", "outline", "transition"],
    "tech": ["css"],
    "era": "2010s",
    "difficulty": "beginner",
    "dependencies": [],
    "browser_support": "All modern browsers.",
    "performance_notes": "Animates transform on a pseudo-element — GPU-friendly.",
    "accessibility_notes": "Real <button>; color contrast valid in both states. Transition removed under prefers-reduced-motion (instant fill).",
    "customization": [
      { "name": "--fill", "description": "Sweep color", "default": "#7c5cff" },
      { "name": "--direction", "description": "transform-origin for the sweep", "default": "left" }
    ],
    "variations": ["Sweep from bottom (origin: bottom, scaleY)", "Diagonal sweep with skew", "Two-color split sweep"],
    "related": ["gradient-border-button", "underline-grow-link"],
    "ai_usage": "Classic ghost-to-solid CTA. Button has transparent bg + colored border + label. A ::before is absolutely positioned, scaleX(0) with transform-origin:left, and animates to scaleX(1) on hover; put label text above it with position:relative;z-index:1 and switch its color. Change transform-origin and the scale axis to control sweep direction."
  }
  </script>
  <style>
    :root { --fill: #7c5cff; }
    html, body { height: 100%; margin: 0; }
    body { display: grid; place-items: center; background: #0b0d12; font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; }
    .sweep-btn {
      position: relative; overflow: hidden; cursor: pointer; isolation: isolate;
      background: transparent; border: 2px solid var(--fill); color: var(--fill);
      padding: 0.8rem 2.2rem; border-radius: 10px; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 600;
      transition: color .35s ease;
    }
    .sweep-btn::before {
      content: ""; position: absolute; inset: 0; background: var(--fill); z-index: -1;
      transform: scaleX(0); transform-origin: left; transition: transform .35s ease;
    }
    .sweep-btn:hover { color: #fff; }
    .sweep-btn:hover::before { transform: scaleX(1); }
    @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .sweep-btn, .sweep-btn::before { transition: none; } }
  </style>
</head>
<body>
  <button class="sweep-btn">Explore</button>
</body>
</html>

effects.title: Glow Pulse Button

effects.description: Animates an expanding box-shadow halo (and subtle scale) in a gentle loop to create an attention-seeking 'breathing' glow around the button.
effects.performance_notes: Animating box-shadow repaints; acceptable for a single hero CTA, avoid many on screen at once.
effects.accessibility_notes: Attention pulse can be distracting; it stops under prefers-reduced-motion. Don't use more than one pulsing element per view.
effects.ai_usage: Use sparingly for the single most important CTA. Apply .pulse-btn; the keyframes animate box-shadow from a tight ring to a wide soft halo and back. Match --glow to your brand accent. Strongly prefer disabling after first interaction or after a few seconds to avoid annoyance; the reduced-motion guard is mandatory.

effects.title: Loading Button
effects.summary: A submit button that swaps its label for an inline spinner and locks during async work.
effects.description: Toggling a loading state hides the label, shows a spinner, disables the button, and sets aria-busy — the correct pattern for form submits and async actions.
effects.performance_notes: One spinner rotation while loading; trivial.
effects.accessibility_notes: Set disabled + aria-busy='true' during loading so it can't be double-submitted and assistive tech knows it's working. Keep the button width stable to avoid layout shift. Restore state on completion/error.
variations: Disable the whole form during submit
effects.ai_usage: Use for any button that triggers async work (submit, save, pay). On action: add the loading class, set button.disabled=true and aria-busy='true', swap label for a spinner; on resolve/reject, revert and optionally show success/error. Reserve a fixed min-width so the button doesn't resize. NEVER leave it stuck loading — always restore on both success and failure paths. The demo auto-toggles to show both states.

effects.title: Material Ripple Button
effects.summary: A circular ripple expands from the click point, the classic Material Design touch feedback.
effects.description: On pointer down, a span is positioned at the cursor and scaled up while fading out, creating the ink-ripple. Vanilla JS, no framework.
effects.performance_notes: Each click spawns one short-lived element that is removed on animationend — no leaks.
effects.accessibility_notes: Purely additive visual feedback over a real <button>; keyboard activation still works. Honors prefers-reduced-motion by skipping the ripple.
variations: Slower ripple for a softer feel
effects.ai_usage: Attach to any real <button>. The JS computes click coordinates relative to the button, sizes the ripple to cover it (diameter = max(width,height)), and removes the node on animationend. Requires position:relative + overflow:hidden on the button. Reuse the addRipple() function for multiple buttons by delegating or looping over a selector.
effects.source:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
  <title>Material Ripple Button</title>
  <script type="application/json" id="effect-meta">
  {
    "id": "ripple-button",
    "title": "Material Ripple Button",
    "summary": "A circular ripple expands from the click point, the classic Material Design touch feedback.",
    "description": "On pointer down, a span is positioned at the cursor and scaled up while fading out, creating the ink-ripple. Vanilla JS, no framework.",
    "theme": "buttons",
    "categories": ["button", "feedback", "interaction"],
    "tags": ["ripple", "material", "click", "feedback", "wave"],
    "tech": ["css", "js"],
    "era": "2010s",
    "difficulty": "intermediate",
    "dependencies": [],
    "browser_support": "All modern browsers.",
    "performance_notes": "Each click spawns one short-lived element that is removed on animationend — no leaks.",
    "accessibility_notes": "Purely additive visual feedback over a real <button>; keyboard activation still works. Honors prefers-reduced-motion by skipping the ripple.",
    "customization": [
      { "name": "--ripple", "description": "Ripple ink color", "default": "rgba(255,255,255,.6)" },
      { "name": "background", "description": "Button base color", "default": "#7c5cff" }
    ],
    "variations": ["Centered ripple for icon buttons", "Dark ripple on light buttons", "Slower ripple for a softer feel"],
    "related": ["gradient-border-button", "press-3d-button"],
    "ai_usage": "Attach to any real <button>. The JS computes click coordinates relative to the button, sizes the ripple to cover it (diameter = max(width,height)), and removes the node on animationend. Requires position:relative + overflow:hidden on the button. Reuse the addRipple() function for multiple buttons by delegating or looping over a selector."
  }
  </script>
  <style>
    html, body { height: 100%; margin: 0; }
    body { display: grid; place-items: center; background: #0b0d12; font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; }
    .ripple-btn {
      --ripple: rgba(255,255,255,.6);
      position: relative; overflow: hidden;
      border: none; cursor: pointer;
      padding: 0.95rem 2.4rem; border-radius: 12px;
      font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 600; color: #fff;
      background: #7c5cff; box-shadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(124,92,255,.4);
    }
    .ripple-btn span.ink {
      position: absolute; border-radius: 50%; transform: scale(0);
      background: var(--ripple); pointer-events: none;
      animation: ripple 0.6s linear;
    }
    @keyframes ripple { to { transform: scale(2.5); opacity: 0; } }
    @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ripple-btn span.ink { display: none; } }
  </style>
</head>
<body>
  <button class="ripple-btn" id="btn">Click me</button>
  <script>
    function addRipple(e) {
      var btn = e.currentTarget;
      var rect = btn.getBoundingClientRect();
      var size = Math.max(rect.width, rect.height);
      var ink = document.createElement("span");
      ink.className = "ink";
      ink.style.width = ink.style.height = size + "px";
      ink.style.left = (e.clientX - rect.left - size / 2) + "px";
      ink.style.top = (e.clientY - rect.top - size / 2) + "px";
      btn.appendChild(ink);
      ink.addEventListener("animationend", function () { ink.remove(); });
    }
    document.getElementById("btn").addEventListener("click", addRipple);
  </script>
</body>
</html>

effects.title: Dark / Light Mode Tokens
effects.summary: A theming pattern where semantic tokens flip between light and dark via a data-attribute — live toggle.
effects.description: Demonstrates the standard themeable token setup: semantic CSS variables defined for the default theme and overridden under [data-theme='light'] / prefers-color-scheme, with a working toggle button.
effects.performance_notes: Swapping a data attribute re-resolves variables instantly; negligible.
effects.accessibility_notes: Respect prefers-color-scheme as the default; let users override via the toggle and persist their choice. Maintain contrast in BOTH themes. The toggle is a real button with aria-pressed.
effects.ai_usage: The canonical dark/light theming pattern. Define semantic tokens on :root for one theme and override the SAME token names under [data-theme='...'] (and/or @media (prefers-color-scheme)). Components only reference semantic tokens, so theme switching is a single attribute change on <html>. Default to the system preference, allow a toggle (button with aria-pressed), and persist the choice in localStorage. Verify contrast in every theme.
effects.source:
Themeable surface

Same tokens, two themes.

effects.title: Gradient Library
effects.summary: A curated board of reusable linear/radial/conic gradients with their exact CSS.
effects.description: A reference palette of production-ready gradients (sunsets, brand blends, mesh, conic) you can copy directly. Each tile shows the gradient and is annotated with its declaration.
effects.browser_support: All modern browsers; conic-gradient is widely supported.
effects.performance_notes: Static backgrounds; free.
effects.accessibility_notes: When placing text over a gradient, check contrast against the lightest stop, or add a scrim.
effects.ai_usage: A copy-paste gradient reference. Pick a tile and lift its background declaration, or store the whole set as tokens (--grad-sunset: linear-gradient(...)). Linear for directional washes, radial for spotlights/blobs, conic for rings/loaders/color wheels. For text, combine with -webkit-background-clip: text. Always scrim text over gradients for contrast.

effects.title: Semantic Color Tokens
effects.summary: A two-tier color token system (primitive scale → semantic roles) as copy-ready CSS custom properties.
effects.description: A reference specimen, not an animation: defines a primitive color scale (--blue-50…900 etc.) and maps it to semantic roles (--color-bg, --color-text, --color-primary, --color-danger…). This is the backbone of a themeable design system.
effects.performance_notes: Variables only; no runtime cost.
effects.accessibility_notes: Token pairs are chosen for WCAG contrast (text-on-bg, text-on-primary). When adding tokens, verify contrast ratios (>=4.5:1 for body text).
variations: Generate the scale with oklch() for perceptual uniformity
effects.ai_usage: This is a copy-paste DESIGN TOKEN foundation, not a visual effect. Lift the :root block into your project. Use the TWO-TIER pattern: primitives (--blue-500) hold raw values; semantic tokens (--color-primary: var(--blue-500)) express intent and are what components reference. Never hardcode hex in components — reference semantic tokens so themes/dark-mode swap cleanly. Verify contrast for every text/bg pairing you introduce.

effects.title: Cursor Spotlight Reveal
effects.summary: The cursor carries a spotlight that reveals hidden content through a dark mask.
effects.description: A full-screen overlay uses a radial-gradient mask centered on the cursor, so only a circle around the pointer is illuminated — a flashlight/torch reveal over otherwise dimmed content.
effects.browser_support: All modern browsers (radial-gradient mask). Prefix mask for older Safari.
effects.performance_notes: Updates two CSS variables on pointermove; the mask is GPU-composited. Cheap.
effects.accessibility_notes: Hiding content behind a cursor mask is bad for keyboard/screen-reader users and anyone who can't aim precisely — only use for playful, non-essential reveals and provide an accessible alternative.

customization.name: --dim
customization.description: How dark the masked area is

variations: Reveal an image layer instead of text
effects.ai_usage: Playful 'reveal in the dark' microsites and easter eggs — NOT for essential content. A fixed overlay sits above content with background var(--dim) and a radial-gradient mask: mask: radial-gradient(circle var(--r) at var(--x) var(--y), transparent 99%, #000). Update --x/--y on pointermove. Always provide an accessible, non-masked path to the same content.

effects.title: Following Dot & Ring Cursor
effects.summary: A custom cursor: a precise dot plus a larger ring that lags behind with easing.
effects.description: Hides the native cursor and renders two elements — a dot that snaps to the pointer and a ring that eases toward it via a rAF lerp — the signature 'agency' custom cursor.
effects.browser_support: Pointer devices only; on touch there is no cursor (gracefully no-ops). All modern browsers.
effects.performance_notes: One rAF loop updating transforms; cheap. Use transform (not top/left) for smoothness.
effects.accessibility_notes: Never hide the cursor on critical UI without a clear, large custom replacement. Respect prefers-reduced-motion (drop the lag/ease). Ensure focus styles remain for keyboard users.
variations: Grow the ring on hovering links
Magnetic ring that snaps to buttons
effects.ai_usage: Expressive marketing sites. Render a fixed dot and ring; on pointermove store target x/y; in a rAF loop snap the dot and lerp the ring (current += (target-current)*ease). Use transform: translate3d for compositing. Add cursor:none on the body but KEEP it usable: enlarge the ring on interactive elements and never hide cursor on forms. Skip the lag under reduced motion. No-op on touch.
effects.source: Move your cursor

effects.title: Gooey Blob Trail Cursor
effects.summary: A soft blurred blob chases the cursor with easing, leaving a liquid trailing feel.
effects.description: A blurred radial blob is eased toward the pointer each frame (lerp), so it lags and stretches subtly — a playful, organic cursor companion.
effects.browser_support: Pointer devices; modern browsers. No-ops on touch.
effects.performance_notes: One rAF loop moving a single blurred element via transform; cheap. Blur is the main cost — keep it moderate.
effects.accessibility_notes: Decorative only; does NOT replace the system cursor here (kept visible), so usability is preserved. Stop the lag/motion under prefers-reduced-motion.
effects.ai_usage: Playful decorative cursor companion (keep the real cursor visible for usability). Render a fixed blurred radial blob; store the pointer target on pointermove and lerp the blob toward it in a rAF loop (current += (target-current)*ease) using transform: translate. Lower ease = more lag/trail. Skip the animation under reduced motion and on touch. Keep blur moderate for perf.
effects.source: Move your cursor around

effects.title: Animated Checkbox
effects.summary: A checkbox whose tick draws itself in with an SVG stroke animation on check.
effects.description: A real checkbox controls an SVG checkmark whose stroke-dashoffset animates from hidden to drawn when :checked, plus a filling box — a satisfying, accessible custom checkbox.
effects.performance_notes: Animates stroke-dashoffset + background; trivial.
effects.accessibility_notes: Backed by a real <input type=checkbox> kept focusable (opacity:0, not display:none). The SVG is decorative (aria-hidden). Keep a visible focus ring and a real <label>.
variations: Radio-button variant with a draw-in dot
effects.ai_usage: Use for multi-select lists, terms acceptance, todo items. Hide the native checkbox (opacity:0) but keep it in flow for focus/keyboard; draw a custom box + inline SVG checkmark. On input:checked, fill the box and animate the SVG path's stroke-dashoffset from path-length to 0 to 'draw' the tick. Keep the real <input> + <label for> and a focus-visible ring.

effects.title: Custom Range Slider
effects.summary: A styled range input with a gradient-filled track that follows the thumb.
effects.description: Styles the native <input type=range> thumb and track cross-browser, and uses a CSS variable updated on input to paint the filled portion of the track up to the current value.
effects.browser_support: All modern browsers; thumb/track styling needs ::-webkit-slider-thumb and ::-moz-range-thumb.
effects.performance_notes: Updates one CSS var on input; trivial.
effects.accessibility_notes: Built on a real <input type=range>, so it is keyboard-operable (arrows) and announces value. Provide a <label> and aria-valuetext if the raw number needs units/context.
variations: Show a value tooltip above the thumb
effects.ai_usage: Use for volume, price, opacity, etc. Keep the native <input type=range> for accessibility; style ::-webkit-slider-thumb and ::-moz-range-thumb plus the track. For the filled portion, set a --fill percentage from JS on 'input' (value normalized to 0-100%) and paint the track with a linear-gradient hard-stop at --fill. Always pair with a <label>.
effects.source:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
  <title>Custom Range Slider</title>
  <script type="application/json" id="effect-meta">
  {
    "id": "custom-range-slider",
    "title": "Custom Range Slider",
    "summary": "A styled range input with a gradient-filled track that follows the thumb.",
    "description": "Styles the native <input type=range> thumb and track cross-browser, and uses a CSS variable updated on input to paint the filled portion of the track up to the current value.",
    "theme": "forms-inputs",
    "categories": ["form", "slider", "control"],
    "tags": ["range", "slider", "input", "fill", "thumb", "accessible"],
    "tech": ["css", "js"],
    "era": "2020s",
    "difficulty": "intermediate",
    "dependencies": [],
    "browser_support": "All modern browsers; thumb/track styling needs ::-webkit-slider-thumb and ::-moz-range-thumb.",
    "performance_notes": "Updates one CSS var on input; trivial.",
    "accessibility_notes": "Built on a real <input type=range>, so it is keyboard-operable (arrows) and announces value. Provide a <label> and aria-valuetext if the raw number needs units/context.",
    "customization": [
      { "name": "--accent", "description": "Filled track + thumb color", "default": "#7c5cff" },
      { "name": "--track", "description": "Empty track color", "default": "#2a3142" }
    ],
    "variations": ["Show a value tooltip above the thumb", "Stepped/ticked slider", "Dual-thumb range (needs two inputs)"],
    "related": ["before-after-slider", "toggle-switch"],
    "ai_usage": "Use for volume, price, opacity, etc. Keep the native <input type=range> for accessibility; style ::-webkit-slider-thumb and ::-moz-range-thumb plus the track. For the filled portion, set a --fill percentage from JS on 'input' (value normalized to 0-100%) and paint the track with a linear-gradient hard-stop at --fill. Always pair with a <label>."
  }
  </script>
  <style>
    :root { --accent: #7c5cff; --track: #2a3142; --fill: 50%; }
    html, body { height: 100%; margin: 0; }
    body { display: grid; place-items: center; background: #0b0d12; font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; }
    .range {
      -webkit-appearance: none; appearance: none; width: 280px; height: 8px; border-radius: 999px; outline: none;
      background: linear-gradient(90deg, var(--accent) var(--fill), var(--track) var(--fill));
    }
    .range::-webkit-slider-thumb { -webkit-appearance: none; appearance: none; width: 22px; height: 22px; border-radius: 50%;
      background: #fff; border: 3px solid var(--accent); cursor: pointer; box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,.5); }
    .range::-moz-range-thumb { width: 22px; height: 22px; border-radius: 50%; background: #fff; border: 3px solid var(--accent); cursor: pointer; }
    .range:focus-visible { box-shadow: 0 0 0 4px color-mix(in srgb, var(--accent) 35%, transparent); }
  </style>
</head>
<body>
  <input class="range" type="range" min="0" max="100" value="50" aria-label="Volume" id="r" />
  <script>
    var r = document.getElementById("r");
    function paint() { r.style.setProperty("--fill", ((r.value - r.min) / (r.max - r.min) * 100) + "%"); }
    r.addEventListener("input", paint); paint();
  </script>
</body>
</html>

effects.title: Floating Label Input
effects.summary: A label that sits inside the field and floats up when focused or filled — pure CSS, fully accessible.
effects.description: Uses an empty placeholder plus :placeholder-shown and :focus to animate a real <label> from inside the input to above it, keeping the label available to assistive tech.
effects.performance_notes: Static + a transform transition; trivial.
effects.accessibility_notes: Critically: keep a REAL <label for> — do not use a placeholder as the only label. The input needs placeholder=' ' (a space) so :placeholder-shown works. Focus styles are visible.
effects.ai_usage: Use for any text field. Markup: wrapper > input(placeholder=' ') + label(for=id). The label is absolutely positioned over the input; when the input :is(:focus, :not(:placeholder-shown)) the adjacent label (input + label) shrinks and moves up. ALWAYS keep the <label> element for accessibility; the placeholder must be a single space, not the field name. Add aria-describedby for error text.

effects.title: Toggle Switch
effects.summary: An iOS-style on/off switch built on a real checkbox for full accessibility.
effects.description: Styles a visually-hidden <input type=checkbox> as a sliding track + knob using :checked, so it stays keyboard-operable, focusable, and form-submittable.
effects.performance_notes: Transform/background transition on :checked; trivial.
effects.accessibility_notes: Built on a real checkbox inside a <label>, so it's keyboard toggle-able (Space), focusable, and announced as a checkbox/switch. Keep a visible focus ring. Add role=switch + aria-checked if you want it announced as a switch.
variations: Icons inside the knob (sun/moon for theme)
Label text that changes with state
effects.ai_usage: Use for boolean settings (NOT for navigation or immediate actions — use a button for those). Wrap a real <input type=checkbox> in a <label> with a slider <span>; hide the native box (opacity:0, not display:none, to keep focus) and style the span via input:checked + .slider. Keep the focus-visible ring. For semantics, add role='switch' and sync aria-checked, or rely on the native checkbox role.

effects.title: Claymorphism Card
effects.summary: A puffy, candy-like 3D clay surface with big radii and inset highlights.
effects.description: Combines very large border-radius, a flat fill, a soft drop shadow, and two inset shadows (a bright top-inner and a darker bottom-inner) to make elements look like inflated soft clay.
effects.performance_notes: Static multi-shadow; trivial.
effects.accessibility_notes: Generally good contrast since fills are solid and bold; keep text legible on the chosen fill.
effects.ai_usage: Playful, friendly UIs (education, kids, casual apps). Recipe: large border-radius, solid fill, an outer drop shadow for lift, plus TWO inset shadows — a light one (top-left, simulating a highlight) and a darker tinted one (bottom-right). Increase radius and shadow softness for puffier clay. Pairs well with bright, saturated palettes.
effects.source: Clay

effects.title: Glass Button
effects.summary: A frosted translucent button with a light top-edge highlight that brightens on hover.
effects.description: Combines a translucent fill, backdrop blur, an inset top highlight, and a subtle border to make a glass button that sits naturally over colorful/photo backgrounds.
effects.browser_support: All modern browsers; backdrop-filter needs -webkit- in Safari.
effects.performance_notes: backdrop-filter has a cost; fine for a few buttons. Provide a solid fallback where unsupported.
effects.accessibility_notes: Translucency can hurt label contrast over busy backgrounds — keep the tint dark enough and test. Real button with a focus ring.
effects.ai_usage: Use over imagery/gradients (glass needs something behind it to blur). Translucent background + backdrop-filter blur + 1px light border + an inset top highlight (box-shadow inset 0 1px rgba(255,255,255,.3)). Include -webkit-backdrop-filter and a solid fallback. Verify label contrast against the worst-case background; keep the focus-visible ring.

      { "name": "--blur", "description": "Backdrop blur", "default": "10px" }

Translucent background + backdrop-filter blur + 1px light border + an inset top highlight (box-shadow inset 0 1px rgba(255,255,255,.3)). Include -webkit-backdrop-filter and a solid fallback. Verify label contrast against the worst-case background; keep the focus-visible ring."
  }
  </script>
  <style>
    :root { --glass: rgba(255,255,255,.12); --blur: 10px; }
    html, body { height: 100%; margin: 0; }
    body { display: grid; place-items: center; font-family: system-ui, sans-serif;

effects.title: Glassmorphism Card
effects.summary: A frosted-glass panel that blurs whatever sits behind it, with a subtle light border.
effects.description: Combines a translucent background, backdrop-filter: blur(), a 1px light border, and a soft shadow to create the frosted-glass surface popularized by iOS/macOS and Windows Acrylic.
effects.browser_support: All modern browsers; backdrop-filter needs the -webkit- prefix for Safari.
effects.performance_notes: backdrop-filter blur is GPU-intensive; limit the blurred area and avoid stacking many glass layers.
effects.accessibility_notes: Translucency can wreck text contrast — keep the tint dark/opaque enough and test contrast over busy backgrounds. Provide a solid fallback where backdrop-filter is unsupported.
effects.ai_usage: Use over a colorful/photographic background (it has nothing to blur over a flat color). Apply .glass: semi-transparent background + backdrop-filter:blur(var(--blur)) + a light 1px border + soft shadow. Always include -webkit-backdrop-filter. Verify text contrast; if backdrop-filter is unsupported, fall back to a higher-opacity solid background.

effects.title: Neumorphic Button
effects.summary: A soft-UI button that appears extruded at rest and presses inward when active.
effects.description: Uses the dual light/dark shadow neumorphism technique; on :active the shadows flip to inset so the button looks physically depressed.
effects.performance_notes: Dual shadows + an inset swap on press; trivial.
effects.accessibility_notes: Neumorphism's classic weakness is low contrast — make the label clearly readable and don't rely only on the shadow to signal it's a button. Keep a visible focus ring (neumorphism often forgets this).

customization.name: --bg
customization.description: Shared surface color (button + page must match)

variations: Toggle that stays pressed when active
effects.ai_usage: Use in calm, tactile soft-UI dashboards — but ensure contrast and an explicit focus ring (neumorphism's main accessibility pitfalls). The button and its container MUST share --bg. Rest: box-shadow with a light offset (top-left) and dark offset (bottom-right). On :active: switch both to inset. Derive the light/dark shades from --bg. Add a focus-visible outline for keyboard users.

Rest: box-shadow with a light offset (top-left) and dark offset (bottom-right). On :active: switch both to inset. Derive the light/dark shades from --bg. Add a focus-visible outline for keyboard users."

effects.title: Neumorphism Card
effects.summary: A soft, extruded 'soft UI' surface made from twin light and dark shadows.
effects.description: Uses two box-shadows — a light one top-left and a dark one bottom-right, both the same hue as the background — so elements look pressed out of (or into) the surface. Includes a pressed (inset) variant.
effects.performance_notes: Static dual shadows; trivial. The :active inset swap is cheap.
effects.accessibility_notes: Notorious for poor contrast — the trend's biggest weakness. Ensure text and interactive states have sufficient contrast; don't rely on shadow alone to convey affordance.
effects.ai_usage: Use for calm, tactile dashboards/players — but audit contrast carefully (this is the trend's main risk). The element and its container MUST share the same --bg. Raised: box-shadow: dist dist blur darkShade, -dist -dist blur lightShade. Pressed/input: same offsets but inset. Derive light/dark shades from --bg. Keep distances modest.

effects.title: 3D Tilt Card
effects.summary: A card that tilts in 3D toward the cursor with a moving glare highlight.
effects.description: Tracks the pointer over the card and maps its position to rotateX/rotateY transforms under perspective, plus a radial glare that follows the cursor — the popular 'parallax tilt' interaction.
effects.performance_notes: Updates CSS variables on pointermove; cheap since only transform/background-position change. Throttle via rAF for many cards.
effects.accessibility_notes: Enhancement only; card is fully usable without hover. Tilt is disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.
variations: Add layered children with translateZ for depth
effects.ai_usage: Premium product/feature cards. Wrap the card in a perspective container; on pointermove compute normalized (-0.5..0.5) x/y from the bounding rect and set rotateY = x*maxTilt, rotateX = -y*maxTilt. Reset on pointerleave. The glare is a radial-gradient whose position follows the same coordinates. For grids, reuse one handler via event delegation and rAF-throttle.
effects.source:
Tilt me

Move your cursor

effects.title: Card Lift on Hover
effects.summary: A card rises and casts a deeper shadow on hover — the dependable elevation cue.
effects.description: Combines a translateY lift with a growing, softer box-shadow to convey elevation change, the most common and reliable hover affordance for clickable cards.
effects.performance_notes: Animating box-shadow can be slightly costly; for buttery performance animate a pseudo-element's opacity instead of the shadow itself.
effects.accessibility_notes: Pure visual affordance; transition removed under prefers-reduced-motion. Pair with :focus-visible for keyboard users.
variations: Performance variant: fade a pre-rendered shadow pseudo-element
effects.ai_usage: The safe default hover for any clickable card or tile. Apply .lift-card; on hover translateY(-var(--lift)) and increase the box-shadow. Always add the same treatment to :focus-visible so keyboard users get the cue. For grids of many cards, prefer the pseudo-element opacity technique to avoid box-shadow repaint cost.
effects.source:

effects.title: Grayscale to Color on Hover
effects.summary: A muted grayscale image blooms into full color on hover — great for logo walls and team grids.
effects.description: Applies filter: grayscale(1) at rest and transitions to grayscale(0) (optionally with a slight scale) on hover/focus, drawing attention to the hovered item.
effects.performance_notes: Animating filter is GPU-accelerated in modern browsers; fine for a grid of moderate size.
effects.accessibility_notes: Also trigger on :focus-within so keyboard users get the effect. Color must not be the only way information is conveyed.
variations: Whole grid muted except the hovered item
effects.ai_usage: Use for logo walls, partner grids, and team photos to create focus on hover. Rest: filter: grayscale(var(--rest)); hover/focus-within: grayscale(0) + optional scale. Trigger on :focus-within too for keyboard users. For a 'spotlight' grid, mute the whole container and only un-mute the hovered child. Replace the gradient placeholders with real + alt text.

effects.title: Image Zoom & Caption Reveal
effects.summary: On hover the image zooms within its frame while a caption slides up from below.
effects.description: An overflow-hidden frame clips an image that scales on hover; a caption bar translates up into view simultaneously — the staple gallery/portfolio thumbnail interaction.
effects.performance_notes: Animates transform on the image and caption — GPU-friendly. Uses a CSS gradient as a stand-in image so the specimen needs no asset.
effects.accessibility_notes: Caption content should also be available without hover (e.g. visible on focus); transitions reduce under prefers-reduced-motion.
effects.ai_usage: Portfolio/gallery thumbnails. Structure: a position:relative, overflow:hidden frame containing the (object-fit:cover) and an absolutely-positioned caption translated 100% down. On frame hover, scale the image and translate the caption to 0. Make the caption keyboard-focusable/visible-on-focus for a11y. Replace the gradient placeholder with a real .

effects.title: Magnetic Button
effects.summary: A button that is gently pulled toward the cursor as it approaches, snapping back on exit.
effects.description: Listens for pointer movement within a hit area and translates the button a fraction of the cursor offset, giving a magnetic, springy feel common in award-winning agency sites.
effects.browser_support: All modern browsers with a pointer; on touch it simply does nothing special.
effects.performance_notes: Transform-only updates on pointermove; smooth via a CSS transition for snap-back.
effects.accessibility_notes: Pure enhancement; button works normally. Disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.
variations: Magnetize the label separately from the button for layered pull
Stronger pull for big hero CTAs
effects.ai_usage: Use for hero CTAs and nav items on expressive, agency-style sites. Compute the cursor offset from the button center and translate the button by offset * strength on pointermove; reset transform to 0 on pointerleave (a CSS transition makes the snap-back springy). Keep strength ~0.2-0.4. Skip on touch and under reduced-motion.
effects.source:
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
  <meta charset="UTF-8" />
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1" />
  <title>Magnetic Button</title>
  <script type="application/json" id="effect-meta">
  {
    "id": "magnetic-button",
    "title": "Magnetic Button",
    "summary": "A button that is gently pulled toward the cursor as it approaches, snapping back on exit.",
    "description": "Listens for pointer movement within a hit area and translates the button a fraction of the cursor offset, giving a magnetic, springy feel common in award-winning agency sites.",
    "theme": "hover-interactions",
    "categories": ["hover", "button", "micro-interaction"],
    "tags": ["magnetic", "cursor", "spring", "agency", "playful", "translate"],
    "tech": ["css", "js"],
    "era": "2020s",
    "difficulty": "intermediate",
    "dependencies": [],
    "browser_support": "All modern browsers with a pointer; on touch it simply does nothing special.",
    "performance_notes": "Transform-only updates on pointermove; smooth via a CSS transition for snap-back.",
    "accessibility_notes": "Pure enhancement; button works normally. Disabled under prefers-reduced-motion.",
    "customization": [
      { "name": "--strength", "description": "Pull factor (0-1)", "default": "0.4" }
    ],
    "variations": ["Magnetize the label separately from the button for layered pull", "Stronger pull for big hero CTAs", "Add scale on hover"],
    "related": ["tilt-card", "spotlight-card"],
    "ai_usage": "Use for hero CTAs and nav items on expressive, agency-style sites. Compute the cursor offset from the button center and translate the button by offset * strength on pointermove; reset transform to 0 on pointerleave (a CSS transition makes the snap-back springy). Keep strength ~0.2-0.4. Skip on touch and under reduced-motion."
  }
  </script>
  <style>
    html, body { height: 100%; margin: 0; }
    body { display: grid; place-items: center; background: #0b0d12; font-family: system-ui, sans-serif; }
    .magnetic {
      border: none; cursor: pointer; color: #fff; font-weight: 700; font-size: 1.05rem;
      padding: 1.1rem 2.6rem; border-radius: 999px; background: #7c5cff;
      transition: transform .3s cubic-bezier(.2,.8,.2,1);
    }
    @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .magnetic { transition: none; } }
  </style>
</head>
<body>
  <button class="magnetic" id="btn">Magnetic</button>
  <script>
    var btn = document.getElementById("btn");
    var STRENGTH = 0.4;
    var reduce = matchMedia("(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)").matches;
    if (!reduce) {
      btn.addEventListener("pointermove", function (e) {
        var r = btn.getBoundingClientRect();
        var x = e.clientX - (r.left + r.width / 2);
        var y = e.clientY - (r.top + r.height / 2);
        btn.style.transform = "translate(" + x * STRENGTH + "px," + y * STRENGTH + "px)";
      });
      btn.addEventListener("pointerleave", function () { btn.style.transform = "translate(0,0)"; });
    }
  </script>
</body>
</html>

effects.title: Spotlight Hover Card
effects.summary: A soft radial spotlight follows the cursor across the card surface.
effects.description: Tracks the pointer and updates CSS variables for a radial-gradient glow that reveals as the cursor moves — the interactive card look popularized by modern dev-tool landing pages.
effects.performance_notes: Only updates two CSS variables on pointermove; very cheap. Scales to a grid with one delegated handler.
effects.accessibility_notes: Decorative enhancement; content fully readable without it. No motion concerns (no looping animation).
variations: Border-only spotlight (mask the glow to the edge)
Group hover: highlight only the hovered card in a grid
effects.ai_usage: Excellent for feature/pricing card grids. Set --x/--y from the cursor position on pointermove and paint a radial-gradient at (var(--x), var(--y)). For a grid, attach the listener to the container and read event.target to update the right card, or update each card from the shared mouse position to get the 'group spotlight' effect. No reduced-motion concern but keep it subtle.

effects.title: Before / After Slider
effects.summary: Drag a handle to wipe between two images — the classic comparison slider.
effects.description: Two stacked images; a range input drives a CSS variable that clips the top image and positions a draggable divider, revealing the 'before' beneath the 'after'.
effects.performance_notes: Updates a CSS variable + clip on input; trivial.
effects.accessibility_notes: Built on a real <input type=range> so it's keyboard-operable and labelable — much better than a custom drag handler. Provide aria-label.
effects.ai_usage: Use for retouch/edit comparisons, product configurators, and map overlays. Stack two images; the top one is clipped via inset(0 calc(100% - var(--pos)) 0 0) (or clip-path). Drive --pos from a real range input's value (keyboard accessible) rather than custom mouse drag. Position the divider/handle at --pos. Replace the placeholder gradients with real before/after images and set alt text.

effects.title: Clip-Path Shapes
effects.summary: Crop images/elements into hexagons, blobs, and diagonals with clip-path, animated on hover.
effects.description: Demonstrates clip-path polygon/inset cropping and a smooth hover morph between two shapes, the basis for diagonal section dividers and decorative image framing.
effects.browser_support: All modern browsers. Animating between polygons requires the same number of points.
effects.performance_notes: Clip-path is GPU-friendly; animating it is smooth if point counts match.
effects.accessibility_notes: Cropping may hide image content; keep important subjects within the visible region. No motion concern beyond the optional hover.
variations: Diagonal section divider (inset/polygon on a band)
Morph between two polygons on hover
effects.ai_usage: Use for decorative image framing, diagonal section dividers, and badges. Apply clip-path to the element; common shapes: hexagon polygon, diagonal polygon for dividers, circle()/ellipse(). To animate between shapes on hover, both clip-paths must have the SAME number of points. Keep key subject matter away from clipped edges.

effects.title: Duotone Image
effects.summary: Recolor any photo into a two-tone gradient using blend modes.
effects.description: Desaturates the image and overlays a two-color gradient with mix-blend-mode so shadows take one color and highlights another — the Spotify-style duotone, done in CSS.
effects.browser_support: All modern browsers (mix-blend-mode, filter). For pixel-perfect duotone use SVG feColorMatrix.
effects.performance_notes: Blend modes + grayscale filter are GPU-accelerated; fine for hero images, avoid on large galleries.
effects.accessibility_notes: Heavy recoloring can hurt recognizability and contrast of any text baked into the image; keep alt text accurate.
effects.ai_usage: Brand-consistent photo treatment for heroes and cards. Wrap the image; apply filter:grayscale(1) contrast(1.1) to the ; overlay a ::after with linear-gradient(var(--shadow),var(--highlight)) and mix-blend-mode: screen (plus a multiply layer for richer results). Pick two brand colors (dark shadow, light highlight). For exact control, switch to an SVG feColorMatrix/feComponentTransfer filter.

effects.title: Ken Burns Effect
effects.summary: A slow zoom-and-pan over a still image that adds cinematic life to hero photos.
effects.description: Animates a slow scale + translate on an image inside an overflow-hidden frame, the documentary 'Ken Burns' pan-and-zoom, ideal for hero backgrounds and slideshows.
effects.performance_notes: Animating transform (scale/translate) is GPU-friendly. Slightly over-size the image so panning never reveals edges.
effects.accessibility_notes: Ambient motion; disable under prefers-reduced-motion (show a static frame). Keep overlaid text on a scrim for contrast.
variations: Crossfade between multiple images for a slideshow
Pan in different directions per slide
Pair with a duotone or gradient overlay
effects.ai_usage: Use for hero backgrounds and photo slideshows to add subtle motion. Put the image in an overflow:hidden frame, scale it slightly above 1 so there's room to move, and animate transform between two scale+translate states over a long duration (15-25s) with ease-in-out alternate. Add a dark scrim/gradient if text sits on top. Disable under reduced motion. Replace the gradient placeholder with a real .

effects.title: Bento Grid
effects.summary: The modern 'bento box' feature grid — tiles of varying sizes in a tidy CSS Grid.
effects.description: Uses CSS Grid with explicit column/row spans so feature tiles form an asymmetric-but-aligned bento layout, the dominant marketing/feature-section pattern of the 2020s.
effects.performance_notes: Pure layout; free.
effects.accessibility_notes: Source order should match reading order; use grid placement, not visual reordering that breaks DOM order for keyboard/screen-reader users.
variations: Collapse to single column on mobile
effects.ai_usage: Use for feature/overview sections and dashboards. Define a grid (e.g. repeat(4,1fr) with auto rows) and give standout tiles grid-column: span N / grid-row: span M. Keep DOM order = reading order. On small screens, drop to one or two columns (the spans should gracefully reflow — set them in a min-width media query). Replace placeholder tiles with real feature content.

effects.title: Holy Grail Layout
effects.summary: Header, footer, fluid main content, and two fixed sidebars — the classic app shell, in CSS Grid.
effects.description: The famously-hard-in-the-old-days 'holy grail' layout (sticky header/footer, flexible center, fixed-width side rails) expressed cleanly with grid-template-areas.
effects.performance_notes: Pure layout; free.
effects.accessibility_notes: Use landmark elements (header/nav/main/aside/footer). Keep DOM order sensible (main content early) for screen readers even if visually centered.
variations: Collapse rails to a single column on mobile
Replace a rail with a collapsible drawer

Assign each landmark element to its area. On mobile, redefine the areas to stack into one column inside a media/container query. Keep semantic landmarks for accessibility.

effects.title: Masonry Grid (CSS columns)
effects.summary: A Pinterest-style staggered masonry layout using CSS multi-columns — no JS.
effects.description: Uses the CSS columns property with break-inside: avoid to flow items into balanced vertical columns of varying heights, the simplest dependency-free masonry.
effects.browser_support: All browsers (CSS columns). Note: items flow top-to-bottom THEN across, so reading order differs from Grid.
effects.performance_notes: Pure CSS; free. (Native CSS Grid masonry is emerging but not yet widely shipped.)
effects.accessibility_notes: Column flow means visual order is column-by-column, which can differ from DOM order expectations — fine for galleries, less ideal for sequential content.
variations: Native grid masonry (grid-template-rows: masonry) where supported
JS libraries for exact left-to-right order
effects.ai_usage: Use for image galleries and pin boards where item heights vary and exact order doesn't matter. Set columns: column-width Npx (fluid) or N (fixed) on the container, column-gap for gutters, and break-inside: avoid + margin-bottom on each item. Caveat: items fill top-to-bottom per column, so it's not left-to-right reading order — use a JS lib or future native grid masonry if order matters.

effects.title: Responsive Card Grid (no media queries)
effects.summary: A card grid that reflows columns automatically using auto-fit + minmax — zero breakpoints.
effects.description: grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(MIN, 1fr)) makes the grid add/remove columns based on available width, the single most useful responsive grid one-liner.
effects.performance_notes: Pure layout; free. No JS or media queries.
effects.accessibility_notes: DOM order = visual order, so tab/reading order is correct by default.

customization.name: min column width
customization.description: The minmax minimum that decides when columns wrap

variations: auto-fill (keeps empty tracks) vs auto-fit (collapses them)
Add minmax(0,1fr) to fix overflow with long content
effects.ai_usage: The default responsive grid for card lists, galleries, product grids. One line: grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(MIN, 1fr)). Choose MIN = the smallest acceptable card width; the grid computes column count per viewport with no media queries. Use auto-fit to collapse empty tracks (cards stretch), auto-fill to keep them. Add minmax(0,1fr) if long words overflow. Resize the frame to watch it reflow.

effects.title: Bouncing Dots
effects.summary: Three dots bounce in sequence — the friendly 'typing…' / loading indicator.
effects.description: Three dots share one bounce keyframe with staggered animation-delays so they rise and fall in a wave, evoking chat 'typing' indicators and lightweight loaders.
effects.performance_notes: Three nodes animating translateY — trivial.
effects.accessibility_notes: Wrap with role=status + a label. Under prefers-reduced-motion, replace bounce with a gentle opacity pulse.
variations: More dots for a longer wave
effects.ai_usage: Use for chat 'typing' states and small inline loaders. Three .dot spans share a bounce animation with delays of 0, 0.16s, 0.32s. Tune --dot color and size. For reduced motion, swap the translateY bounce for an opacity pulse so it still signals activity without movement.
effects.source: Loading…

effects.title: Circular Progress Ring
effects.summary: A determinate SVG ring that fills to a percentage via stroke-dashoffset, with a live label.
effects.description: An SVG circle whose stroke-dasharray equals its circumference; animating stroke-dashoffset reveals the arc proportional to a percentage — the standard circular progress meter.
effects.performance_notes: Animating stroke-dashoffset is cheap. For pure decoration use CSS; for real values update from JS.
effects.accessibility_notes: Add role=progressbar with aria-valuenow/min/max and keep the numeric label as text so the value is announced. Not just color — the number conveys the state.
effects.ai_usage: Use for upload/download progress, scores, and timers. circumference = 2*pi*r; set the circle's stroke-dasharray to that, and stroke-dashoffset = circumference * (1 - pct). Rotate the circle -90deg so it starts at top. Update offset + the text label + aria-valuenow from JS as progress changes. The demo animates 0→100% on a loop.
effects.source: 0%

effects.title: Dual Ring / Orbit Spinner
effects.summary: Two concentric arcs spin in opposite directions for a more dynamic loader.
effects.description: A container and a pseudo-element each render an arc (colored border on opposing sides) and counter-rotate, producing an orbiting double-ring spinner.
effects.performance_notes: Two rotating layers, transform-only — cheap.
effects.accessibility_notes: Pair with role=status + label. Slow down under prefers-reduced-motion.
effects.ai_usage: A slightly fancier spinner for splash/loading screens. Outer element has border-top-color:--c1 and spins one way; ::after is an inset ring with border-bottom-color:--c2 spinning the other way. Keep within role=status and a hidden label. Size via --size.
effects.source: Loading…

effects.title: Indeterminate Progress Bar
effects.summary: A sliding accent segment loops across a track when progress can't be measured.
effects.description: A track contains a short bar that animates its position/width across, signaling ongoing work of unknown duration — the top-of-page loading bar pattern.
effects.performance_notes: Animates transform/left of one segment; cheap.
effects.accessibility_notes: Use role="progressbar" with aria-valuetext or an adjacent 'Loading' label; omit aria-valuenow when indeterminate. Animation continues under reduced-motion but you may slow it.
variations: Fix to top of viewport for route changes
effects.ai_usage: Use for unknown-duration tasks and page/route transitions (often pinned to the top of the viewport). The track is overflow:hidden; an inner segment animates from left:-40% to left:100%. Switch to a real determinate <progress> or a width-driven bar once you know the percentage. Set role=progressbar and a label.

effects.title: Ring Spinner
effects.summary: The classic spinning ring with a single colored arc, drawn with one element.
effects.description: A circular element with a transparent border on three sides and a colored top border spins continuously — the most recognizable loading indicator.
effects.performance_notes: Animating rotation via transform is GPU-friendly. One DOM node.
effects.accessibility_notes: Add role="status" and an aria-label/visually-hidden 'Loading' for screen readers. The spin itself is essential feedback, but pair with a text label.
variations: Two arcs (top+bottom) for a denser look
Dashed border for a tick ring
Slow spin for a calmer feel
effects.ai_usage: Default spinner for async actions and page/section loads. Apply .spinner; the colored border-top-color + transparent other sides creates the arc, animation:spin 1s linear infinite rotates it. Always wrap with role=status and a visually-hidden 'Loading…' label. Size via --size; match --color to brand.
effects.source: Loading…

effects.title: Skeleton Loader
effects.summary: Gray placeholder blocks with a sweeping shimmer that mimic content while it loads.
effects.description: Content-shaped gray boxes get a moving highlight gradient (the same trick as shimmer text) to signal that real content is on its way — reducing perceived load time.
effects.performance_notes: Animates background-position only; cheap even for many blocks.
effects.accessibility_notes: Mark the region aria-busy="true" and/or role=status so assistive tech announces loading. Shimmer stops under prefers-reduced-motion (static gray).
variations: Pulse opacity instead of shimmer sweep
effects.ai_usage: Show in place of content during data fetches; match block shapes/sizes to the real layout to avoid reflow jank. Apply .skeleton to each placeholder; it tiles a 3-stop gradient (base, sheen, base) at background-size:200% and animates background-position. Set aria-busy on the container, then swap in real content when loaded.

effects.title: Easing Curve Showcase
effects.summary: Side-by-side comparison of common easing curves, animated, with their cubic-bezier tokens.
effects.description: A reference specimen running the same movement through several named easings (ease-out, ease-in-out, a snappy back-ease, etc.) so you can feel the difference and copy the cubic-bezier values as motion tokens.
effects.performance_notes: A handful of transform loops; trivial.
effects.accessibility_notes: Demonstration motion stops under prefers-reduced-motion. In real UIs, prefer ease-out for entrances and keep durations short.
variations: Pair each ease with a recommended duration
effects.ai_usage: A motion-token reference. Store these as --ease-out, --ease-in-out, --ease-emphasized, --ease-back tokens and reference them in transitions/animations for consistency. Rules of thumb: ease-out for elements entering/responding to input (fast start, gentle settle), ease-in for exits, ease-in-out for movement between two on-screen states, linear ONLY for continuous motion (spinners). Avoid the default 'ease' for everything. Always pair with a reduced-motion guard.

--ease-in-out
